title: Context window & context management
intro: Quick check after the sections above.
feedbackIncorrect: Review the context window and context management sections — try again if needed.

questions.prompt: In LLMs, what does the term "context window" refer to?
choices: The maximum amount of text (tokens) the model can take in and use at once for a turn
The physical size of the chat panel in your IDE
The number of users who can share one API key

questions.prompt: What happens to content that falls outside the model's context window?
choices: It is silently merged into the system prompt
It cannot affect the response unless you bring it back into the window
It is always retrieved later from long-term memory

questions.prompt: Context management in this course suggests aiming to use roughly how much of the available context window?
choices: As close to 100% as possible so nothing is left unused
Less than about 40%, keeping only what matters most in view

questions.prompt: What is a primary goal of good context management?
choices: To prevent important details from being buried under irrelevant or outdated information
To ensure every file in the repo is pasted into every prompt
To replace the need for clear instructions in your prompts
